assumptions: The developer has a supported browser.
No Clerk account, Dashboard application, SDK, or authentication identifier set has been chosen yet.

developer_goal: Create a Clerk account and application, integrate Clerk into an application stack, and reach the first signed-in user state.

first_success.outcome: A first test user completes sign-up in the integrated app and the signed-in profile control appears.
first_success.observable_signal: The profile icon is visible in place of Sign in and Sign up.

route_selection.surface: Clerk Dashboard account setup plus framework Quickstarts
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after one SDK stack and Account Portal versus components versus custom flow model are fixed.

candidates.name: Next.js App Router quickstart
candidates.reason: Next.js is one of many full-stack quickstarts and itself has App Router versus Pages Router variants.

candidates.name: Other full-stack or frontend SDK quickstarts
candidates.reason: Clerk's Quickstarts index lists multiple peer stacks such as React, Expo, Vue, Express, and native SDKs.

candidates.name: Clerk CLI agent-assisted init versus manual SDK setup
candidates.reason: Current Next.js docs emphasize clerk auth login and clerk init, which is a different surface from manual package and file edits.

required_path.action: Create a Clerk account or sign into the Clerk Dashboard.

required_fields.label: Clerk account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Setup docs link to account creation but do not enumerate current required fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer reaches the Clerk Dashboard or the first-application setup form.

required_path.action: Create a Clerk application and choose sign-up and sign-in identifiers and social providers.

required_fields.label: Authentication identifiers and social providers
required_fields.notes: Exact required defaults versus optional toggles are not fully enumerated without the live setup form.

required_path.observable_result: A Clerk application exists with API keys available in the Dashboard.

required_path.action: Integrate Clerk using the selected stack quickstart or CLI init path.
required_path.observable_result: The local app runs with Clerk sign-in and sign-up controls visible.

required_path.action: Sign up as the first test user in the running application.

required_fields.label: First test user credentials for the configured identifiers

required_path.observable_result: The profile icon replaces Sign in and Sign up.

prerequisites.description: A Clerk Dashboard application must exist before SDK keys can be copied into a project.
An application stack must be chosen because Quickstarts are stack-specific.

platform_outcomes.description: Clerk creates the application and issues publishable and secret API keys.
Clerk creates the first user after successful in-app sign-up.

excluded.item: Reading Core concepts and AI prompt guides as measured actions
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is excluded from required developer actions.

excluded.item: Organizations and component customization after first user
excluded.reason: Official Next.js after-setup recommendations come after the first signed-in profile state.

sources.title: Quickstarts
evidence_supported: Multiple full-stack, frontend, and backend peer quickstarts

sources.title: Add Clerk Authentication to Next.js
evidence_supported: Clerk CLI login and init path for Next.js

uncertainties.question: Which Clerk SDK stack and Account Portal versus components versus custom-flow model define first success?
uncertainties.impact: Stack and UI model change dependencies, files, environment keys, and verification steps.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose one bounded quickstart such as Next.js App Router with prebuilt components, then verify Dashboard setup through first profile icon.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields appear on current Clerk account signup and the interactive authentication setup form?
uncertainties.impact: Unverified signup and identifier defaults block a verified field count.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account capture of dashboard.clerk.com signup and Create application setup.
